title:
Assembly election LIVE: 116 candidates, including CM, Speaker, LoP file nominations in Assam
 till Friday
content:
A total of 116 candidates have filed their nominations for the Assam Assembly election.
*   This group includes prominent political figures such as the Chief Minister, Speaker, and Leader of Opposition.
*   The nominations were filed up until Friday, marking a significant phase in the election process.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Manjeswaram set for another photo finish as key players line up
content:
The Manjeswaram constituency in Kerala is predicted to have a "photo finish" – a very close contest – in the 2026 Assembly Elections.
*   The term "another" implies this constituency has a history of extremely tight electoral battles.
*   This anticipated closeness is attributed to prominent key players and candidates beginning to line up for the election.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: RSP’s battle for survival after a decade of legislative absence
content:
The RSP party is facing a critical challenge in the lead-up to the Kerala Assembly Elections in 2026.
*   Their upcoming electoral performance is being framed as a direct "battle for survival" for the party.
*   This desperate situation is a result of the RSP's complete absence from the state legislature for the past decade.

title: Gujarat, Centre sign MoU under Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0
content:
Gujarat and the Central government have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).
*   This agreement falls specifically under the ambit of the Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0.
*   It signifies a collaborative effort to further the mission's objectives, likely focused on providing safe and adequate drinking water.

title: No queues for LPG as Gujarat assures adequate supply, pushes digital booking
content:
There are currently no queues for LPG in Gujarat.
*   The state government assures that there is an adequate supply of LPG.
*   Gujarat is actively promoting digital booking methods for LPG cylinders.

title: Show cause notice illegal, arbitrary, say BJD MLAs who cross-voted in Rajya Sabha poll
content:
BJD MLAs who cross-voted in the Rajya Sabha poll are challenging the show cause notice issued against them.
*   They assert that the notice is "illegal" and "arbitrary."
*   The notice was served as a disciplinary measure following their act of cross-voting.

title: Redevelopment of Angamaly-Mannuthy NH 544 to be completed before monsoon, Centre tells HC
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   The redevelopment work on the Angamaly-Mannuthy NH 544 is underway.
*   The project is slated for completion before the upcoming monsoon season.
*   This assurance was given by the Centre to the High Court.

title: CAPF Bill says IPS officers’ deputation necessary for Union-State coordination, reserves 50% I-G rank posts for IPS
content:
Here's a summary of the news headline in 3 bullet points:

*   The CAPF Bill emphasizes the necessity of deputing IPS officers to ensure effective coordination between the Union and State governments.
*   It proposes reserving 50% of Inspector General (IG) rank posts within the Central Armed Police Forces specifically for IPS officers.
*   This measure aims to solidify IPS presence at key leadership levels to facilitate seamless inter-governmental police operations.

title: Communists fight elections to reflect rights of people, says Brinda Karat
content:
Communists are actively participating in elections.
*   Their stated purpose for fighting elections is to reflect the rights of the people.
*   This perspective was articulated by Brinda Karat.

title: A true benevolent authoritarian enforces discipline for the greater good: Seeman
content:
Seeman advocates for the concept of a "true benevolent authoritarian."
*   This type of leader would enforce discipline.
*   The purpose of this discipline is to achieve "the greater good."
